title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 6
subtitle: Hard-tier prediction set modelled on 2023 examiner patterns
instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.
Difficulty is benchmarked at or above the actual 2023 paper: expect multi-step questions.

questions.text: A's income is 20% more than B's, and B's income is 25% less than C's. A's income is what percentage of C's income?
A trader marks an article at Rs 1300 and allows a 10% discount, still gaining 17%. The cost price of the article is:
A can do a work in 12 days and B in 15 days. They work on alternate days, starting with A. The work is completed in:
questions.explanation: 12 days finish 9/10; A's 13th day leaves 1/60, which B does in 1/4 day.

questions.text: Two trains running in opposite directions cross a man standing on the platform in 27 seconds and 17 seconds respectively, and they cross each other in 23 seconds. The ratio of their speeds is:
A boat whose speed in still water is 9 km/h goes 12 km upstream and returns to the start in a total of 3 hours. The speed of the stream is:

A vessel contains 80 litres of pure milk. 8 litres are drawn out and replaced with water; then 8 litres of the mixture are drawn out and replaced with water again. The quantity of milk now in the vessel is:
The simple interest on a sum for 2 years at 10% per annum is Rs 1200. The compound interest on the same sum at the same rate for the same period is:
A and B start a business with Rs 45000 and Rs 60000. After 4 months, C joins with Rs 90000. Out of a year-end profit of Rs 44000, C's share is:
questions.explanation: Capital-months 540:720:720 = 3:4:4; C gets 4/11 of 44000.

questions.text: Enter the largest three-digit number which, when divided by 6, 9 and 12, leaves a remainder of 3 in each case.
questions.explanation: Largest 3-digit multiple of 36 is 972; add 3.

questions.text: The sum of the present ages of a father and son is 60 years. Six years ago, the father's age was five times the son's age. The son's age 6 years from now will be:
questions.explanation: Six years ago the ages were 8 and 40; son is now 14, so 20 later.

questions.text: Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 and 30 minutes. Both are opened together, but A is shut after some time, and the tank is filled in a total of 18 minutes. After how many minutes was A shut?
A man spends 60% of his income. His income increases by 25% and his expenditure increases by 20%. The percentage increase in his savings is:
questions.explanation: Savings go from 40 to 125 - 72 = 53, i.e. 32.5% up.

questions.text: A solid metallic cone of base radius 6 cm and height 24 cm is melted and recast into identical solid spheres of radius 2 cm. The number of spheres formed is:
The area of the region bounded by the line 2x + y = 8 and the two coordinate axes is:
questions.explanation: Intercepts 4 and 8; area = (1/2) x 4 x 8 = 16.

questions.text: Two parallel chords of lengths 30 cm and 16 cm lie on the same side of the centre of a circle of radius 17 cm. The distance between the chords is:
questions.explanation: Distances from centre are 8 and 15; difference is 7.

questions.text: Sentence 1: The Green Revolution transformed Indian agriculture in the 1960s. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: This success, however, came at the cost of depleted aquifers and degraded soils. Q: High-yield seeds, fertilisers and assured irrigation raised wheat output dramatically. R: Punjab and Haryana, the revolution's showcase states, now face both problems acutely. S: India moved from ship-to-mouth imports to comfortable buffer stocks within a decade.
questions.explanation: Mechanism, outcome, hidden cost, present-day example.

questions.text: Sentence 1: The Indian Constitution is often called a living document. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Judicial interpretation has likewise expanded rights the framers never spelt out. Q: Parliament has amended it more than a hundred times to meet new needs. R: Together, amendment and interpretation keep the text in step with society. S: Its framers deliberately made it neither too rigid nor too flexible.
questions.explanation: Design intent, amendment, interpretation, combined effect.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To ride roughshod over

options.text: To treat someone harshly without regard for their feelings
To ride a horse without a saddle
To escape from a difficult situation

questions.explanation: It means to domineer over someone inconsiderately.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To be at daggers drawn

options.text: To be bitterly hostile towards each other
To be ready with a solution

questions.explanation: It describes open enmity between two parties.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the phrase: A Pyrrhic victory

options.text: A victory gained at too great a cost
A victory in the final battle

questions.explanation: The winner's losses outweigh the gain.

questions.text: One who compiles dictionaries:
questions.explanation: A lexicographer compiles dictionaries.

questions.text: The study or collection of coins:
questions.explanation: Numismatics deals with coins and currency.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: Connoisseur: double n, double s.

questions.text: Select the incorrectly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is supersede.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: No sooner did he arrive WHEN the meeting began.
questions.explanation: 'No sooner' is always paired with 'than'.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: It is high time we START working on the project.
questions.explanation: 'It is high time' takes the past subjunctive.

questions.text: Select the synonym of OBDURATE:
questions.explanation: Obdurate means stubbornly refusing to change.

questions.text: Select the antonym of PROFLIGATE:
questions.explanation: Profligate (recklessly extravagant) is opposite to thrifty.

questions.passage: The Antarctic ice sheet holds enough frozen water to raise global sea levels by nearly sixty metres. For decades scientists believed it would respond only sluggishly to warming, but satellite records now show ice loss tripling since the 1990s. The greatest anxiety centres on West Antarctica, where glaciers rest on bedrock below sea level; warm ocean currents can melt them from beneath, a process that may become self-sustaining once begun. Even a partial collapse would redraw coastlines over centuries. Researchers stress that the timing remains uncertain, yet they agree that the choices made this century will decide the outcome.
questions.text: According to the passage, West Antarctica causes particular concern because:

options.text: It holds most of the continent's ice
Its glaciers sit on bedrock below sea level, exposed to warm currents from beneath
Its ice has already fully collapsed

questions.explanation: The grounding below sea level allows melting from below.

questions.passage: The Antarctic ice sheet holds enough frozen water to raise global sea levels by nearly sixty metres. For decades scientists believed it would respond only sluggishly to warming, but satellite records now show ice loss tripling since the 1990s. The greatest anxiety centres on West Antarctica, where glaciers rest on bedrock below sea level; warm ocean currents can melt them from beneath, a process that may become self-sustaining once begun. Even a partial collapse would redraw coastlines over centuries. Researchers stress that the timing remains uncertain, yet they agree that the choices made this century will decide the outcome.
questions.text: Which of the following is NOT stated in the passage?

options.text: Ice loss has tripled since the 1990s
The ice sheet could raise sea levels by about sixty metres
East Antarctica is melting the fastest
A partial collapse would redraw coastlines

questions.explanation: The passage never mentions East Antarctica.

questions.passage: The Antarctic ice sheet holds enough frozen water to raise global sea levels by nearly sixty metres. For decades scientists believed it would respond only sluggishly to warming, but satellite records now show ice loss tripling since the 1990s. The greatest anxiety centres on West Antarctica, where glaciers rest on bedrock below sea level; warm ocean currents can melt them from beneath, a process that may become self-sustaining once begun. Even a partial collapse would redraw coastlines over centuries. Researchers stress that the timing remains uncertain, yet they agree that the choices made this century will decide the outcome.
questions.text: In the passage, 'self-sustaining' most nearly means:

options.text: Continuing on its own once started

questions.explanation: The melting would feed itself once triggered.

questions.text: Find the next term: 6, 13, 28, 59, ?
questions.explanation: Pattern x2+1, x2+2, x2+3, then 59 x 2 + 4.

questions.text: Find the next term: 2, 9, 28, 65, 126, ?
Find the next term: 7, 8, 18, 57, 232, ?
Find the next term: AZB, CYD, EXF, ?
questions.explanation: Outer letters run A,B,C,D... while the middle runs Z,Y,X,W.

questions.text: In the arrangement 7 % A 9 # B C $ 2 @ 6 D * E, how many symbols are immediately preceded by a digit and immediately followed by a letter?
questions.explanation: Only % (7-%-A) and # (9-#-B) qualify.

questions.text: If CLOUD is coded as 59672 and RAIN as 8341, then AROUND is coded as:
If PAPER is coded as SDSHU, how is PENCIL coded?
questions.explanation: Every letter moves 3 places forward.

questions.text: In a code language, 'morning walk is good' = 'na pa ta la', 'walk keeps body fit' = 'ja pa da ra' and 'good for body' = 'la ra sa'. What is the code for 'morning'?
questions.explanation: walk = pa and good = la; 'morning' and 'is' cannot be separated.

questions.text: Seven friends J, K, L, M, N, O, P sit in a row facing north. J is at the extreme left and K is fourth to the right of J. M sits exactly midway between J and K. N is immediately to the right of K. L is at the extreme right. O is immediately to the left of M. Who sits fourth from the right end?
questions.explanation: Order is J, O, M, P, K, N, L; fourth from the right is P.

questions.text: Six friends P, Q, R, S, T, U sit around a circular table. P sits between Q and R, U sits between R and T, and S sits between T and Q. Who sits directly opposite P?
questions.explanation: Circle order Q, P, R, U, T, S puts T opposite P.

questions.text: In a row, Ajay is 17th from the left and Vijay is 19th from the right. After they interchange positions, Ajay becomes 25th from the left. What is Vijay's new position from the right?
questions.explanation: Total = 25 + 19 - 1 = 43; Vijay is now 17th from left = 27th from right.

questions.text: What day of the week was 26 January 1950, the day the Indian Constitution came into force?
questions.explanation: Republic Day 1950 fell on a Thursday.

questions.text: A man said to a woman, 'Your mother's husband's sister is my aunt.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: Her father's sister is his aunt too, so they share a father.

questions.text: P x Q means P is the mother of Q; P / Q means P is the husband of Q; P + Q means P is the brother of Q. In A / B x C + D, how is A related to D?
questions.explanation: B is mother of C and D; A, her husband, is D's father.

questions.text: Ravi walks 3 km north, turns right and walks 4 km, turns right again and walks 3 km, then turns left and walks 2 km. How far and in which direction is he from the start?
questions.explanation: North legs cancel; east legs total 4 + 2 = 6 km.

questions.text: If 'x' means '/', '/' means '-', '-' means '+' and '+' means 'x', then 16 x 4 / 2 - 8 + 3 = ?
Find the odd one out: 121, 169, 225, 289, 324
questions.explanation: All others are squares of odd numbers; 324 = 18^2.

questions.text: Statements: Some files are folders. No folder is a drawer. Conclusions: I. Some files are not drawers. II. No file is a drawer.
questions.explanation: Files that are folders cannot be drawers; II is too strong.

questions.text: Select the figure that continues the series, in which elements rotate and one element is replaced at each step.

figure.label: Figural Series
figure.description: Four figures where shapes rotate 90 degrees clockwise and the innermost element changes alternately; the fifth figure must be chosen.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual series question.

questions.text: Select the correct mirror image of the given alphanumeric cluster when the mirror is placed to the right.

figure.label: Mirror Image
figure.description: A mixed cluster of letters and digits whose right-side mirror image must be identified from four closely similar options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual mirror-image question.

questions.text: Which country topped the medal tally at the Paris 2024 Olympics after a tie of 40 golds was broken on total medals?
questions.explanation: The USA and China had 40 golds each; the USA had more total medals.

questions.text: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 (Baker, Hassabis, Jumper) recognised work on:

options.text: Protein structure prediction and computational protein design

questions.explanation: It honoured AlphaFold and computational protein design.

questions.text: ISRO's SpaDeX docking mission was launched in December 2024 aboard which rocket?
questions.explanation: PSLV-C60 carried the two SpaDeX satellites.

questions.text: In the Union Budget 2025-26, income up to what annual amount became effectively tax-free under the new regime (with rebate)?
questions.explanation: The rebate threshold was raised to Rs 12 lakh.

questions.text: Who presided over the Lahore Session of the Indian National Congress (1929) that adopted the Purna Swaraj resolution?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru presided at Lahore in December 1929.

questions.text: The Vernacular Press Act (1878), which curbed Indian-language newspapers, was enacted under:
questions.explanation: Lytton passed it; Ripon later repealed it.

questions.text: The Mullaperiyar Dam, subject of a long dispute between Tamil Nadu and Kerala, stands on which river?
questions.explanation: It is built on the Periyar river in Kerala.

questions.text: The Ratle hydroelectric project, often in the news amid Indus Waters Treaty tensions, is being built on which river?
questions.explanation: Ratle is on the Chenab in Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.text: In October 2024, how many additional languages were granted classical language status by the Union Cabinet?
questions.explanation: Marathi, Pali, Prakrit, Assamese and Bengali were added.

questions.text: The Uniform Civil Code is mentioned in which Article of the Constitution, under the Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to secure a UCC.

questions.text: 'The Golden Road: How Ancient India Transformed the World' (2024) is authored by:
questions.explanation: Dalrymple's 2024 book traces India's ancient influence.

questions.text: Anura Kumara Dissanayake was elected President in September 2024 in which neighbouring country?
questions.explanation: He won Sri Lanka's presidential election.

questions.text: Khel Ratna 2024 awardee Harmanpreet Singh captains India in which sport?
questions.explanation: He led the hockey team to Olympic bronze in Paris.

questions.text: The Unified Lending Interface (ULI), piloted from 2024 as a 'UPI for credit', is an initiative of:
questions.explanation: The RBI is building ULI for frictionless credit.

questions.text: The interplanetary mission approved by the Union Cabinet in 2024 for launch later this decade will orbit which planet?
questions.explanation: The Venus Orbiter Mission (Shukrayaan) was approved.

questions.text: The 'One Nation One Subscription' scheme approved in November 2024 provides nationwide access to:
questions.explanation: It gives institutions access to scholarly journals.

questions.text: In Excel, A1 = 5, A2 = 8, A3 = 9. What does =IF(SUM(A1:A3)>20, MAX(A1:A3), MIN(A1:A3)) return?
questions.explanation: Sum is 22 (>20), so MAX = 9 is returned.

questions.text: In VLOOKUP, setting the fourth argument (range_lookup) to FALSE forces:

options.text: A search in the last column

questions.explanation: FALSE (or 0) demands an exact match.

questions.text: In MS Word, Ctrl + Shift + C copies:

options.text: The formatting of the selected text

questions.explanation: It copies formatting, applied later with Ctrl + Shift + V.

questions.text: In Windows, the shortcut Windows key + Shift + S is used to:
questions.explanation: It opens the Snipping tool overlay.

questions.text: Which of the following memories is the fastest?
questions.explanation: Registers inside the CPU are the fastest storage.

questions.text: Cache memory is generally built from:
questions.explanation: Static RAM is faster and used for cache.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT utility software?
questions.explanation: A DBMS is application/system software, not a utility.

questions.text: The program that combines separately compiled object modules into a single executable is the:
questions.explanation: The linker joins object files into one executable.

questions.text: Assembly language programs are translated into machine code by:
questions.explanation: An assembler handles assembly-to-machine translation.

questions.text: The Von Neumann architecture is characterised by:

options.text: Separate memories for data and instructions
Storing data and instructions in the same memory
The absence of any stored program

questions.explanation: It uses a single memory for program and data.
